description_long: This object is a small, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic low-wing configuration. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the lower part of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two seats. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel, characteristic of a taildragger configuration. The exterior surface is metallic with a dark, possibly gray or black, finish.
description: A small, low-wing, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined metallic body, conventional tail, and fixed taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, forward cockpit, and straight low-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the streamlined fuselage, fixed main and tail landing gear, and conventional tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the tail wheel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit likely configured for one or two seats.
